name: Convolver
description: This algorithm performs real-time convolution of the input signal and another signal (often called an 'impulse response'), which is loaded from the MicroSD card. This is often used for realistic reverb effects, since the required impulse responses can be sampled from a physical location, but the technique is much more general and can be a very creative way to mangle and combine sounds. Convolution is notoriously CPU-intensive, and the disting NT is a resource-constrained embedded system – you should not expect to achieve the same results as you might in the latest new fangled convolution reverb plug-in in your DAW, with orders of magnitude more CPU power and RAM. However, this algorithm remains a useful tool within the context of a modular synth.
gui_description: The display shows a graphical representation of the impulse sample on the left of the screen. On the right side, it shows the mono/stereo processing mode, the maximum impulse time (as a result of the current parameter values), the actual length of the sample file, and the current overall CPU usage.

specifications.name: Max impulse
specifications.description: The maximum length of the impulse sample.

parameters.name: Folder
parameters.description: Chooses the folder from which to load the impulse response sample.

parameters.name: Sample
parameters.description: Chooses the impulse response sample within the folder.

parameters.name: Sample rate
parameters.description: Sets the processing sample rate (48kHz, 24kHz, 12kHz, or 6kHz).

parameters.name: Partitions
parameters.description: Allows you to fine tune the maximum impulse length, and consequently the CPU usage.

parameters.name: Dry gain
parameters.description: The output level of the dry signal.

parameters.name: Convolution gain
parameters.description: The output level of the convolution result.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono audio input bus.
